controls.title: Ensure filesystem integrity checking is configured
controls.description: File integrity monitoring (FIM) software should be used to verify that critical OS files have not been altered.
controls.remediation: Install and configure AIDE or Tripwire for filesystem integrity monitoring.

controls.title: Ensure permissions on bootloader config are not group or world writable
controls.description: The grub configuration file contains information on boot settings and passwords. It must be protected from unauthorized modification.
controls.remediation: Run: chmod og-rwx /boot/grub/grub.cfg

controls.title: Ensure time synchronization is in use
controls.description: Time synchronization is important to support time sensitive security mechanisms and ensure audit records are consistent across the enterprise.
controls.remediation: Install and enable either NTP or chrony for time synchronization.

controls.title: Ensure X Window System is not installed
controls.description: The X Window System provides a Graphical User Interface (GUI) where users can have multiple windows. Unless required, it should not be installed on servers.
controls.remediation: Remove X Window packages: apt-get remove xorg

controls.title: Ensure IP forwarding is disabled
controls.description: The net.ipv4.ip_forward flag is used to tell the system whether it can forward packets or not. If this system is not intended to be a router, disable forwarding.
controls.remediation: Set net.ipv4.ip_forward = 0 in /etc/sysctl.conf

controls.title: Ensure broadcast ICMP requests are ignored
controls.description: Setting net.ipv4.icmp_echo_ignore_broadcasts to 1 will cause the system to ignore all ICMP echo and timestamp requests to broadcast/multicast addresses.
controls.remediation: Set net.ipv4.icmp_echo_ignore_broadcasts = 1 in /etc/sysctl.conf

controls.title: Ensure auditd is installed
controls.description: auditd is the userspace component to the Linux Auditing System. It is responsible for writing audit records to disk.
controls.remediation: Install auditd: apt-get install auditd

controls.title: Ensure audit log storage size is configured
controls.description: Configure the maximum size of the audit log file.
controls.remediation: Set max_log_file = <MB> in /etc/audit/auditd.conf

controls.title: Ensure password creation requirements are configured
controls.description: Strong passwords protect systems from being hacked through brute force methods. Set minimum password length and complexity requirements.
controls.remediation: Configure /etc/pam.d/common-password with pam_pwquality settings.

controls.title: Ensure lockout for failed password attempts is configured
controls.description: Lock out users after n unsuccessful consecutive login attempts. The first sets of actions are, delay in seconds following failed attempts.
controls.remediation: Configure pam_tally2 or pam_faillock in /etc/pam.d/common-auth.

controls.title: Ensure SSH root login is disabled
controls.description: The PermitRootLogin parameter specifies if the root user can log in using ssh. Disable this to prevent direct root logins.
controls.remediation: Set PermitRootLogin no in /etc/ssh/sshd_config

controls.title: Ensure SSH Protocol is set to 2
controls.description: SSH Protocol 1 is a legacy protocol that contains security vulnerabilities. Only SSHv2 should be used.
controls.remediation: Set Protocol 2 in /etc/ssh/sshd_config

controls.title: Ensure SSH PermitEmptyPasswords is disabled
controls.description: SSH PermitEmptyPasswords must be disabled to prevent unauthenticated access.
controls.remediation: Set PermitEmptyPasswords no in /etc/ssh/sshd_config

controls.title: Ensure a firewall is installed and active
controls.description: A host-based firewall should be installed and active to limit network exposure.
controls.remediation: Enable and configure ufw or iptables firewall.

controls.title: Ensure default deny firewall policy
controls.description: A default deny all policy on connections ensures that any unconfigured network usage will be rejected.
controls.remediation: Set default deny policy: ufw default deny incoming

controls.title: Ensure IPv6 is disabled if not required
controls.description: If IPv6 is not to be used, it is recommended that it be disabled to reduce the attack surface of the system.
controls.remediation: Add ipv6.disable=1 to GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX in /etc/default/grub if IPv6 is not needed.

controls.title: Ensure web server is not installed if not required
controls.description: Web servers (Apache, nginx) should only be installed if needed. Unnecessary services expand the attack surface.
controls.remediation: Remove web server if not required: apt-get remove apache2 nginx

controls.title: Ensure TLS 1.2 or higher is used
controls.description: TLS 1.0 and 1.1 are deprecated protocols with known vulnerabilities. Only TLS 1.2 and 1.3 should be used.
controls.remediation: Configure SSLProtocol in web server config to exclude TLSv1 and TLSv1.1.

controls.title: Ensure database default passwords are changed
controls.description: Default database passwords must be changed immediately after installation.
controls.remediation: Change default database passwords using ALTER USER or SET PASSWORD commands.

controls.title: Ensure MFA is enabled for all privileged accounts
controls.description: Multi-factor authentication adds an additional layer of security by requiring a second form of verification.
controls.remediation: Enable MFA for all privileged accounts using an authentication app or hardware token.

controls.title: Ensure least privilege is enforced
controls.description: Users should only have the minimum permissions required to perform their job functions.
controls.remediation: Audit user permissions and remove unnecessary privileges.

controls.title: Ensure inactive accounts are disabled
controls.description: Inactive accounts that have been unused for an extended period should be disabled to reduce risk.
controls.remediation: Disable accounts inactive for more than 30 days.

controls.title: Ensure syslog is configured
controls.description: Syslog should be configured to capture system events and forward them to a central log server.
controls.remediation: Install and configure rsyslog or syslog-ng.

controls.title: Ensure log files are not world-writable
controls.description: Log files should be protected from unauthorized modification.
controls.remediation: Set correct permissions on /var/log: chmod g-wx,o-rwx /var/log/*

controls.title: Ensure audit logs are sent to a remote server
controls.description: Sending audit logs to a remote server ensures logs cannot be tampered with locally.
controls.remediation: Configure auditd to forward logs to a central syslog server.
